events.label: assistant
events.text:
I evaluated the trench protection scenario and wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`.

Key call: **needs_more_info** rather than a straight pass/fail. Pre-task photos showing guards, access control, and crew protection "in place" plus a signed briefing form demonstrate intent, but they don't document the specific data points OSHA 1926 Subpart P requires to actually verify compliance — soil classification, protective system rating matched to depth/soil, spoil pile setback (2 ft minimum), secondary egress spacing (ladder within 25 ft for trenches ≥4 ft), and atmospheric testing where applicable. Without the competent person's inspection log backing up the photos, I can't confirm the setup is actually adequate versus just present.

Listed concrete actions to close the gap (pull the daily inspection log, confirm protective system matches soil/depth tables, verify spoil setback and egress spacing, confirm atmospheric monitoring if needed, and re-authorize entry immediately before occupancy since a pre-task snapshot doesn't certify conditions at time of entry).
